title: Electrical stimulation of temporal and limbic circuitry produces distinct responses in human ventral temporal cortex

funding_references.funder_name: R01 MH122258 CRCNS: Processing speed in the human connectome across the lifespan
T32 GM065841: Medical Scientist Training Program at Mayo Clinic

funding_references.award_title: CRCNS: Processing speed in the human connectome across the lifespan

funding_references.funder_name: National Institute of General Medical Sciences
funding_references.award_title: Medical Scientist Training Program at Mayo Clinic

description: This dataset comprises intracranial EEG recordings from five patients undergoing single-pulse electrical stimulation of temporal and limbic circuitry. The study investigates distinct neural responses in human ventral temporal cortex using direct brain stimulation and electrophysiological recording. Data were collected at Mayo Clinic as part of an NIH-funded investigation of processing speed in the human connectome.
methods_description: Intracranial EEG recordings were obtained during single-pulse electrical stimulation delivered at approximately 0.2 Hz frequency. Stimulation parameters: 200 microsecond duration, biphasic waveform, 6 mA amplitude. Patients were at rest during recording sessions.
